name: Hakka Chinese
notes: Hakka Chinese (Hak-kâ-ngî). The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified and NO logographic map is claimed: Chinese characters do not encode pronunciation segmentally, so a character → IPA table is out of scope for this engine. INPUT CONTRACT: as `zh` requires pinyin, a future Hakka spec would require romanised input in Pha̍k-fa-sṳ (the Hakka missionary romanisation, the Hakka counterpart of Pe̍h-ōe-jī) — or the Taiwanese Hakka Romanization System — not Han characters. Hakka is also strongly dialect-split (Sixian, Hailu, Meixian…), each with its own tone system. Metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode hakk1236), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode hakk1236.

ancestors.notes: Sinitic — the classification node this language sits under.
